---
title: "Digital literacy: Data preparation"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: true
  message: false
---

**Task**: Deidentifies and saves data for replication

# Setup

```{r}
#| label: data-setup-packages

# Load packages
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, haven, knitr)
```

# Paths and labels

```{r}
#| label: data-paths-and-labels

# This file requires access to full data
data_path <- "../../1_Projects/Covid Vaccine Allocation/4_data/"

pref_var <- "party_preference_"   

v_datapaths <- c(
  "wave_9"  = paste0(data_path, "wave_9/Full-Launch/Video_reshaped.dta"),
  "wave_10" = paste0(data_path, "wave_10/Video_reshaped.dta")
)


df_w9 <- read_dta(paste0(
  data_path, 
  "/wave_9/Full-Launch/115522 HU Berlin Vorstudie SCRIPTS Science Impftracker Welle 9 - final2_incl_matching_and_w6_w8_info.dta" ))

# Edit figure labels here
strategies <- 
  c("political\n(emotional)", "political\n(neutral)")

ps <- tibble(
  parties = c("AfD", "BSW", "CDU/CSU", "FDP", "Grüne", "Linke", "SPD"),
  party_stub = c("afd", "bsw", "cdu", "fdp", "gruene", "linke", "spd")
)
```

# Helper functions


```{r}
#| label: data-helper-functions

prepare_data <- function(D) {
  df <- read_dta(v_datapaths[D]) |>
    # Keeping ID which is used for linking periods but no PII
    # Dropping long text fields
    select(-any_of(c("USER", "record", 
                     "videoCurrentTimep1r", "video_desc"))) |>
    filter(SwitchedToRandom == 1) |>
    filter(!duration_video_ %in% c(-9, NA)) |>
    mutate(
      workshop_Treatment = ifelse(workshop_Treatment == 2, 0, as.numeric(workshop_Treatment)),
      strategy_2 = case_when(
        content == "entertainment" ~ "entertainment",
        strategy == "neutral" ~ strategies[2],
        strategy %in% c("anger", "fear", "superiority") ~ strategies[1]
      ),
      party = party |> str_replace("_", "/"),
      partisan = NA
    ) |>
    group_by(ID) |>
    mutate(number_video = n()) |>
    ungroup() |>
    filter(number_video >= 2)

  # Construct partisanship variable
  for (p in 1:nrow(ps)) {
    df <- df |>
      mutate(
        partisan = ifelse(
          party == ps$parties[p],
          case_when(
            # Strong / weak self reported identification
            !!sym(paste0(pref_var, ps$party_stub[p])) == 1 ~ 1,
            TRUE ~ 0
          ),
          partisan
        )
      )
  }

  df
}

```


# Prepare and export data 

This prepares the short-term (wave_9) and long-term (wave_10) datasets a single time.

```{r}
#| label: dataprep
prepare_data("wave_9") |> write_rds("data/wave_9.rds")
prepare_data("wave_10") |> write_rds("data/wave_10.rds")

```


# Retain video descriptions

400 videos 

```{r}
#| label: data-retain-video-descriptions

labels_df <- 
  bind_rows(
    read_dta(v_datapaths[1]) |> select(tiktok_id, video_desc) |> distinct(),
    read_dta(v_datapaths[2]) |> select(tiktok_id, video_desc) |> distinct()) |>
  distinct()

labels_df |> head() |> kable()

```

# Propensity to vote data

```{r}

# Scale 0 - 1
clean_ptv <- function(x, demean = FALSE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- ifelse(x == 99, NA_real_, x)
  if(demean) x <- x - mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  x/10
}

df_w9_ptv <- 
  df_w9 |>
  select(record_w9,
         starts_with("PTV_GLESr"), starts_with("PTV_Posterior"),
         workshop = workshop_Treatment) |>
  mutate(
    ptv_pre_cdu    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr1, demean = TRUE),      # "CDU/ CSU"
    ptv_pre_spd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr3, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_pre_linke  = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr4, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_pre_gruene = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr5, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_pre_fdp    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr6, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_pre_afd    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr7, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_pre_bsw    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr8, demean = TRUE),
    ptv_post_cdu    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr1),   # "CDU/ CSU"
    ptv_post_spd    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr3),
    ptv_post_linke  = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr4),
    ptv_post_gruene = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr5),
    ptv_post_fdp    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr6),
    ptv_post_afd    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr7),
    ptv_post_bsw    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr8))

write_rds(df_w9_ptv, "data/df_w9_ptv.rds")

```

# Attrition data

```{r}
# helper: make 1/0/NA bins from party codes
code_party_bin <- function(x, codes) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  ifelse(is.na(x), NA_integer_, as.integer(x %in% codes))
}

df <- 
  read_dta("../../1_Projects/Covid Vaccine Allocation/5_writing/Paper 11/Stata do-files/Check_longterm_effects/144461 HU Berlin Impftracker Welle 10 - FINAL_Destring_-_Merged_Respondents_Enriched.dta") 


df <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(SwitchedToRandom == 1) |>
    filter(!VideoDuration %in% c(-9, NA)) |>
  mutate(
    # optional readable label
    treated_w9 = treated,
    # attrition indicator
    respondent_type = if_else(`_merge` == 3, 2L, 1L),
    respondent_type = factor(
      respondent_type,
      levels = c(1L, 2L),
      labels = c("only wave 9", "wave 9 and wave 10")
    ),
    # binary indicator: 1 if wave 9 & wave 10, else 0
    w9_w10 = if_else(`_merge` == 3, 1L, 0L),
    # raw cleaned 0–10 PTVs (99 -> NA)
    ptv_cdu_w9_pre    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr1),   # "Die CDU/Die CSU" in your data
    ptv_spd_w9_pre    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr3),
    ptv_linke_w9_pre  = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr4),
    ptv_gruene_w9_pre = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr5),
    ptv_fdp_w9_pre    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr6),
    ptv_afd_w9_pre    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr7),
    ptv_bsw_w9_pre    = clean_ptv(PTV_GLESr8),
    # post-treatment PTVs (wave 9)
    ptv_cdu_w9_post    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr1),   # "Die CDU/ Die CSU"
    ptv_spd_w9_post    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr3),
    ptv_linke_w9_post  = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr4),
    ptv_gruene_w9_post = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr5),
    ptv_fdp_w9_post    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr6),
    ptv_afd_w9_post    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr7),
    ptv_bsw_w9_post    = clean_ptv(PTV_Posteriorr8),
    # Optional: combined party string (useful later)
    party = case_when(
      Parteienidentifikation %in% c(1, 2) ~ "CDU/CSU",
      Parteienidentifikation == 3 ~ "SPD",
      Parteienidentifikation == 4 ~ "AfD",
      Parteienidentifikation == 5 ~ "Gruene",
      Parteienidentifikation == 6 ~ "FDP",
      Parteienidentifikation == 7 ~ "Linke",
      Parteienidentifikation == 8 ~ "BSW",
      Parteienidentifikation == 9 ~ "Other",
      Parteienidentifikation == 10 ~ "None",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Binary indicators (1 if that party, 0 otherwise; NA stays NA)
    party_id_cdu_w9    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation %in% c(1, 2)),
    party_id_spd_w9    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 3),
    party_id_afd_w9    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 4),
    party_id_gruene_w9 = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 5),
    party_id_fdp_w9    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 6),
    party_id_linke_w9  = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 7),
    party_id_bsw_w9    = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 8),
    party_id_other_w9  = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 9),
    party_id_none_w9   = as.integer(Parteienidentifikation == 10)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # optional readable label
    party_w10 = case_when(
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) %in% c(1, 2) ~ "CDU/CSU",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 3 ~ "SPD",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 4 ~ "AfD",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 5 ~ "Gruene",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 6 ~ "FDP",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 7 ~ "Linke",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 8 ~ "BSW",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 9 ~ "Other",
      as.numeric(Parteienidentifikation_W) == 10 ~ "None",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # wave 10 binaries
    party_id_cdu_w10    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, c(1, 2)),
    party_id_spd_w10    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 3),
    party_id_afd_w10    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 4),
    party_id_gruene_w10 = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 5),
    party_id_fdp_w10    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 6),
    party_id_linke_w10  = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 7),
    party_id_bsw_w10    = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 8),
    party_id_other_w10  = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 9),
    party_id_none_w10   = code_party_bin(Parteienidentifikation_W, 10)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Binary: 1 = East, 0 = West, NA = not identified/unknown
    east_bin = case_when(
      state %in% c(3L, 4L, 8L, 13L, 14L, 16L) ~ 1L,     # Berlin, BB, MV, SN, ST, TH
      state == 17L                                   ~ NA_integer_,  # not identified
      is.na(state)                                    ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                                            ~ 0L
    ),
    # Labeled factor for East/West
    east_west = factor(east_bin, levels = c(0L, 1L),
                       labels = c("West", "East")),
    # Labeled factor for Bundesland
    state = factor(
      state,
      levels = 1:17,
      labels = c(
        "Baden-Württemberg", "Bayern", "Berlin", "Brandenburg", "Bremen",
        "Hamburg", "Hessen", "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern", "Niedersachsen",
        "Nordrhein-Westfalen", "Rheinland-Pfalz", "Saarland", "Sachsen",
        "Sachsen-Anhalt", "Schleswig-Holstein", "Thüringen", "not identified"
      )
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # East (Berlin + the five new states are coded as East)
    east = dplyr::case_when(
      state %in% c("Berlin", "Brandenburg", "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern",
                   "Sachsen", "Sachsen-Anhalt", "Thüringen") ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),

    # Specific binary for Saxony
    Saxony = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Sachsen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),

    # --- One dummy per state (0/1/NA) ---
    state_Baden_Wuerttemberg = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Baden-Württemberg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Bayern = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Bayern" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Berlin = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Berlin" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Brandenburg = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Brandenburg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Bremen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Bremen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Hamburg = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Hamburg" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Hessen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Hessen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Mecklenburg_Vorpommern = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Mecklenburg-Vorpommern" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Niedersachsen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Niedersachsen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Nordrhein_Westfalen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Nordrhein-Westfalen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Rheinland_Pfalz = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Rheinland-Pfalz" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Saarland = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Saarland" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Sachsen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Sachsen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Sachsen_Anhalt = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Sachsen-Anhalt" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Schleswig_Holstein = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Schleswig-Holstein" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    ),
    state_Thueringen = dplyr::case_when(
      state == "Thüringen" ~ 1L,
      state == "not identified" ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE ~ 0L
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # make sure it's numeric first (if it came in as labelled/factor)
    urban_num = as.numeric(urban),

    urban_ballungsraum = dplyr::case_when(
      urban_num == 1 ~ 1L,
      urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE           ~ 0L
    ),
    urban_stadtregion = dplyr::case_when(
      urban_num == 2 ~ 1L,
      urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE           ~ 0L
    ),
    urban_mittelzentrum = dplyr::case_when(
      urban_num == 3 ~ 1L,
      urban_num == 4 ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE           ~ 0L
    ),
    # optional: single binary "urban (high density) vs. rest"
    urban_high = dplyr::case_when(
      urban_num %in% c(1, 2) ~ 1L,
      urban_num == 3         ~ 0L,
      urban_num == 4         ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                   ~ NA_integer_
    )
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(-urban_num)  



df <- df %>%
  # your select; removed one duplicate column, kept order stable
  dplyr::select(
    `_merge`, ID_W9, 
    # 
    w9_w10, respondent_type,
    # wave 9 pre covariates
    Alter, Geschlecht, 
    plz, 
    east,
    state_Baden_Wuerttemberg, state_Bayern, state_Berlin, state_Brandenburg,
    state_Bremen, state_Hamburg, state_Hessen, state_Mecklenburg_Vorpommern,
    state_Niedersachsen, state_Nordrhein_Westfalen, state_Rheinland_Pfalz,
    state_Saarland, state_Sachsen, state_Sachsen_Anhalt,
    state_Schleswig_Holstein, state_Thueringen,
    urban_ballungsraum,urban_stadtregion,urban_mittelzentrum,urban_high,
    citysize,
    # social media 
    Video_UserFacebook, Video_UserTikTok, Video_UserSnapchat,
    Video_UserPinterest, Video_UserX_ehem_Twitter, Video_UserTwitch,
    Politikinteresse, Most_important_issue, Competence, LR,
    # party id
    party_id_cdu_w9,party_id_spd_w9,party_id_afd_w9,
    party_id_gruene_w9,party_id_fdp_w9,party_id_linke_w9,
    party_id_bsw_w9,
    # ptv w9 pre 
    ptv_cdu_w9_pre, ptv_spd_w9_pre, ptv_linke_w9_pre, ptv_gruene_w9_pre,
    ptv_fdp_w9_pre, ptv_afd_w9_pre, ptv_bsw_w9_pre, 
    # other
    Prior_performance_general_2, Solidaritat,
    Sozialer_Zusammenhalt, Migration, TikTok_User, TikTok_Potential,
    # treatment
    treated_w9,
    # ptv w9 post 
    ptv_cdu_w9_post, ptv_spd_w9_post, ptv_linke_w9_post, ptv_gruene_w9_post,
    ptv_fdp_w9_post, ptv_afd_w9_post, ptv_bsw_w9_post,
    # job
    Beruflicher_Status, Berufliche_Stellung,
    # education
    Bildung_schulisch, Bildung_beruflich,
    # other
    Eigene_Kinder, HaushaltsgroBe, Migrationshintergrund_Eltern, Haushaltseinkommen,
    # outcomes (W9)
    like_w9, bookmark_w9, share_w9, report_w9,
    # secondary oucomes w9
    # political emotion outcomes if present
    w9_like_political_emotion, w9_book_political_emotion,
    w9_share_political_emotion, w9_report_political_emotion,
    # outcomes (W10) 
    like_w10, bookmark_w10, share_w10, report_w10,
    # party id
    party_id_cdu_w10,party_id_spd_w10,party_id_afd_w10,
    party_id_afd_w10,party_id_gruene_w10,party_id_fdp_w10,
    party_id_linke_w10,party_id_linke_w10,party_id_bsw_w10,
    # outcomes (W10)
    w10_like_political_emotion, w10_book_political_emotion,
    w10_share_political_emotion, w10_report_political_emotion
  ) %>%
  rename_with(tolower)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # TikTok_User (1=Ja, 2=Nein, 3=kenne TikTok nicht)
    tiktok_user_yes = case_when(
      tiktok_user == 1L ~ 1L,
      tiktok_user %in% c(2L, 3L) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    tiktok_user_no = case_when(
      tiktok_user == 2L ~ 1L,
      tiktok_user %in% c(1L, 3L) ~ 0L,
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    # TikTok_Potential (asked only if tik_tok_user == 2)
    # 1=Ja, 2=Nein; NA when not asked (has account or doesn’t know TikTok)
    tiktok_potential_yes = case_when(
      tiktok_user == 2L & tiktok_potential == 1L ~ 1L,
      tiktok_user == 2L & tiktok_potential == 2L ~ 0L,
      tiktok_user %in% c(1L, 3L)                  ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                                          ~ NA_integer_
    ),
    tiktok_potential_no = case_when(
      tiktok_user == 2L & tiktok_potential == 2L ~ 1L,
      tiktok_user == 2L & tiktok_potential == 1L ~ 0L,
      tiktok_user %in% c(1L, 3L)                  ~ NA_integer_,
      TRUE                                          ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )  %>% filter(!is.na(treated_w9)) |>
  mutate(
   attrit = ifelse(w9_w10 == 1, 0, 1) # Define outcome (1 = attrited, 0 = stayed)
   )

df |> write_rds("data/df.rds")

```
